description: This figure presents Mott-Schottky analysis results for Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels (LDSs) with different grain sizes obtained via solution treatment at various temperatures. Panel (a) displays Mott-Schottky curves plotting C⁻² versus potential (E vs. SCE) for four samples: S0 (as-hot rolled, no solution treatment), S950 (950°C), S1050 (1050°C), and S1150 (1150°C). The curves reveal duplex semiconductor characteristics: Region 1 (-1.13 to -0.93 V_SCE) exhibits positive slopes indicating n-type behavior dominated by oxygen vacancies and cation interstitials, while Region 2 (-0.93 to -0.67 V_SCE) shows negative slopes characteristic of p-type behavior governed by cation vacancies. The S950 sample exhibits the highest C⁻² values, while S0 shows the lowest. Panel (b) quantifies the donor (N_D) and acceptor (N_A) concentrations calculated from the Mott-Schottky slopes. S950 achieves the lowest defect densities (N_D = 0.26×10²¹/cm³, N_A = 0.68×10²¹/cm³), significantly lower than S0 (N_D = 0.70×10²¹/cm³, N_A = 2.25×10²¹/cm³). S1050 (N_D = 0.38×10²¹/cm³, N_A = 1.03×10²¹/cm³) and S1150 (N_D = 0.49×10²¹/cm³, N_A = 1.25×10²¹/cm³) show intermediate values. These results demonstrate that grain size optimization via 950°C solution treatment minimizes structural defects in the Al-rich oxide film, thereby reducing chloride ion penetration pathways and enhancing corrosion resistance compared to both the as-rolled and higher-temperature treated samples.
